Throneblade of the Ykesha  
 

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Skill: 2H Slashing Atk Delay: 51
DMG: 33 Dmg Bonus: 170
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +6 INT: +6
Effect: Ykesha (Combat, Casting Time: Instant) at Level 46
WT: 10.0 Size: LARGE
Class: WAR PAL RNG SHD BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

sorry but you are wrong there is still a dmg bonus it.

the price is right
QuoteReply
# Sep 07 2005 at 10:00 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591856936535
1 post
I must of ran into a steal the other day on The Nameless, cause I only paid 10 plat for this thing. I got it for my SK to lvl up his 2h slash and its a beast on the dmg department. I'm not to impressed with the delay, and my lvl isn't high enough for the proc so I can't remark on that. All in all though I think I couldn't of done any better on the price and I recommend this sword if anyone can find it at a decent price.

▲ Page top
whats better?
QuoteReply
# Feb 03 2003 at 3:53 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591783803433
49 posts
sup all,

i have a 19 warrior who i have using dual wield. hes does about 25dmg a slash and has dual wield & double attack. when i use my ykesha (which i got off this cool ranger in kunark, no im not a twink)
i only deal about 30 a slash.

should i keep working with 2hs or use my 1hs

thanks
▲ Page top
RE: whats better?
QuoteReply
# Feb 03 2003 at 10:32 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1594570760601
3 posts
The damage you're seeing with the 2 hander is in direct correlation to your level. When you hit...level 21 I think your damage table changes. What this means is that the minimum and maximum amount of damage you do with a 1 or 2 hander will go up as your level goes up. What most people don't know is that even for dual wielding classes a good 2 hander is better than dual wielding up until about level 45 or so. The reason being is that pre-45 your dual wielding skill is too low to effectively dual wield 2 swords.

Lets compare...

Sword of XXX
20dmg 40dly
1 hand slash

Big Sword of ZZZ
40dmg 40dly
2 hand slash

Now just looking at the above you would think DWing XXX would equal ZZZ since DWing XXX would theoretically put you at 40dmg 40dly. Well what you have to remember is that the offhand misses a lot until you raise your DW skill.

Kinda confusing but just remember that a comparable 2 hander will always outdamage DW 1 hander until you get proficient enough in DWing.
▲ Page top
I dont fully aggree
QuoteReply
# Dec 26 2003 at 8:26 AM Rating: Decent
Valdon
674 posts
I dont fully aggree with your advice. I think your mathmatics are fine, I would not really know. But a few counter points.

1. AC - As a younger warrior one of the best things you can do is get yourself a good shield. Characters under 10 that use a 1HS are not going to be duel weilding. If you have the cash to buy this you can probably get, even something simple like a Velium Round shield. Weight 0.5 15 AC...

2. Delay and Skillset. You are going to swing half as often. This means you are going to skill up much slower, as a twinked Melee, your can cripple your own skills. In an odd way doing insane damage early on, starts bitting you in the butt. Because around 10 you cant hit anything, and you have a monster delay. I think since your capped at 20 (when under 10) you may as well swing as often as you can, with a weapon ratio - combined with Strength that will give you about the same damage.

I have been using an "Ornate miners pic" Its like 12/30 (but yes, I have a "Darkedged two hander" that I DO love...)

And how will he ever gain skill in duel weild without practicing it, it ALWAYS harder later on in the game. Whipping out that weapon on a mob with 15% health, running to get skills.

3. Proc ... I know this sells way cheap, but it also does not proc untill 46. *IF* you have a ton of cash, get that sword that procs at lvl 1. A crypt keepers sword? or something. Its like a 50 some point proc at lvl 1.

I am just saying with damage caps and all, Id rather use my Darkedged two hander, if your talking price. Sure this is great. I mean this weapon is a dream. The augments are nice too. STRG and INT (Nice for a SK)

Just something to Concider. Also concider your Class. As a Ranger they have some really nice 2 handers, but id rather skill up in 1hs, because of the duel weild, double attack. You should concider that also. Pally and SK its all good. But War should try to use many weapons and Ranger should focus on 1hs mostly.

Edited, Fri Dec 26 08:33:01 2003
____________________________
· 'Valdon Gravedancer' Terris-Thule. Dwarven Cleric 34th circle.

· 'Shinook Eldergod' Terris-Thule. Troll Shaman of 32nd rite.
· 'Soultree Vixen' Terris-Thule. Woodelf Ranger of 34th season.

▲ Page top
Twisted Teaser
QuoteReply
# Nov 27 2001 at 2:41 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591771653348
9 posts
My HiE Pally was merely in his 13th season and was paged by a Crusader to come to the Bank at Freeport.

The kind Crusader gave him the Throneblade, amongst other incredible items.

Now I know, I have at least 30 more seasons to go before I can use this weapon to it's full potential, being in my 15th season now.

I enjoy the damage, I am hitting for 30pts per blow. Was only doing about 10 - 12 w/ my longsword. I think however, I will need to switch back to the Long sword and shield, and put this puppy in the bank, till I can use it right. . .
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Feb 27 2002 at 5:21 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) It has nothing to do with seasons, why do people refer to them as that? Hell, I'd have only 8 "seasons" if it had anything to do with that (a year and a half of playing), instead I have 60 LEVELS.
RE: Twisted Teaser
QuoteReply
# Mar 16 2002 at 4:09 PM Rating: Decent
Wulfgarin
Sage
113 posts
Polumbos posts : *It has nothing to do with seasons, why do people refer to them as that? Hell, I'd have only 8 "seasons" if it had anything to do with that (a year and a half of playing), instead I have 60 LEVELS.*


Why worry over trivialities, but since you asked...

sea*son (se'zen), n. 1. one of the four periods of the year (spring, summer, autumn, and winter), beginning astronomically at an equinox or solstice, but geographically at different dates in different climates.

(I assume this is the definition you are referring to, in which case you would be 6 "seasons" having played a year and a half, but how many "seasons" is the character you are playing? 60 Levels in a year and a half of his "life"? I doubt it.=)

Other definitions of season:

7. any period of time: in the season of my youth (or first level perhaps?)
16. to mature, ripen, or condition by exposure to suitible conditions or treatment: a writer seasoned by experience (or perhaps a monk seasoned by exposure to hardship?)
19. to become hardened: troops seasoned by battle (or perhaps a warrior seasoned by battle?)

It would seem that "season" is a perfectly good expression to the amount of experience one has obtained. Why be so caught up in leveling, and lose the flavor of the roleplaying experience? My shaman actually "levels" by achieving his next "circle" of advancement. Thus he is currently in his 40th "circle". My shadow knight has recently earned his 18th "skull". My monk has barely passed his 11th "discipline". And my lowliest of lowlies... my warrior has succeeded in his 7th "season".

Lighten up, use your imagination, and for goodness sake... HAVE FUN !

(definitions courtesy of "Webster's Encyclopedic Unabridged Dictionary of the English Language" *New Deluxe Edition, copyright 1996 by Random House Value Publishing, Inc.)
____________________________
Wulfgarin Dergeistdesmonds
http://www.magelo.com/eq_view_profile.html?num=266075
▲ Page top
RE: Twisted Teaser
QuoteReply
# Apr 11 2002 at 11:14 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592253565546
View User Forum
59 posts
Bravo, bravo my fine shamman. Some of us prefer to roleplay, and by using the term season (a period of learning) it may take us away from the non-roleplaying idea of levels. It is my opinion, that I spent 32 seasons of 7 circles with my druid...not that i obtained lvl 32. I thank you for the wonderful post!

Edited, Thu Apr 11 12:16:48 2002
▲ Page top
RE: Twisted Teaser
QuoteReply
# Sep 07 2002 at 12:18 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591779925142
Scholar
23 posts
Why refer to levels at all while IC? My paladin has no levels, seasons, grades of initiation, or whatever; he is simply a champion of his deity doing what he does as well as he might.

If you want to know what level someone's character is, ask him or her OOC. Experience and/or general capability isn't really ICly quantifiable, so let's stop trying to convince anyone otherwise by veiling the question "What level are you?" in more or less creative phrasing.
▲ Page top
RE: Twisted Teaser
QuoteReply

__DEL__1592248582255

# Oct 23 2001 at 7:43 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592247834587
15 posts
ok to ther person that asked about the Vehement Sword of Reivaj ver. Throneblade of the Ykesha! Hands down gotta go with the Vehemtn Sword of Reivaj i have owned both and currently own both. bought them both at the same time for 7k (for both not a piece) and i love the damage of the Ykesha but you cant beat the proc of the Vehement. its a 300hp(roughly) rune and a 40point str buff. i use the Vehement when im main tank and the Ykesha when im secondary tank

▲ Page top
RE: Bonus?
QuoteReply
# Oct 24 2001 at 11:36 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249404220
4 posts


Bought one of these on Xev last night for 2k.

Best crit so far is 176, best cripple 217. (with 200 str.) Proc's on average 2-3 times a fight with about 100 dex.

Damage bonus is 18.

Seems a little slow compared to some of the other hefty 2 handers out there, but for 2k I think its a steal.

I currently switch out to my STR gear from my ac/hp items when using this, which is exclusively when I am not in a MA or SA role.

With a haste item and a chanter speed buff, this thing is quite serviceable IMO.
▲ Page top
Throneblade of Ykesha
QuoteReply
# Aug 29 2001 at 3:12 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765386487
3 posts
Great weapon for my ranger, especially for times when I need to reduce her taunt ability. If she needs to get her taunt up, I just switch to dual wield.

I think that would make a great weapon for rangers who want to avoid getting hit too often due to their being only a chain class.

Rakaiah
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Nov 06 2001 at 8:35 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) 2HS sucks for rangers because when you get up to levels where it actually matters if you draw the taunt or not there will always be somebody there who is the "main" tank and will keep aggro from you, and if there isnt then you are the "main" tank and will have to KEEP the aggro, i duel wield 11 24 and 14 28, each with respectable bonuses and procs(venemous axe and snare whip), i can keep the taunt when i need to but if not i just stop clicking the "taunt" button, its not difficult, u will learn that when u stop being a noob. and also i have beaten down warriors of the same lvl as myself (51) with plate equip comparable to my chain, so dont think just because we r chain we suck, we used to suck bad, but now we do not contrary to popular belief.
#REDACTED, Posted: Nov 06 2001 at 8:34 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) 2HS sucks for rangers because when you get up to levels where it actually matters if you draw the taunt or not there will always be somebody there who is the "main" tank and will keep aggro from you, and if there isnt then you are the "main" tank and will have to KEEP the aggro, i duel wield 11 24 and 14 28, each with respectable bonuses and procs(venemous axe and snare whip), i can keep the taunt when i need to but if not i just stop clicking the "taunt" button, its not difficult, u will learn that when u stop being a noob. 'nuff said

I just got this thing yesterday, and I must say it's one HELL of a damager...but way to slow for my taste, we SKs really hate to loose our taunt power like this. I was wondering if this thing is worth the trade of a Noctivagant Blade? If so anyone on Torvo server that wishes to trade, contact me sometime.

RE: Trade?
QuoteReply
# Sep 13 2001 at 7:34 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592757765879
Scholar
23 posts
I have two set ups for my SK, and oddly enough, both weapons came from the same mob.

When I'm the primary tank or fighting a caster I use a MoSS and shield for a fast thumping taunt and extra AC. When I'm the secondary tank, I switch out my belt with AC and health stats for an FBSS, and slash away with this thing.

The lower taunt comes in handy when I'm backing up a higher level Warrior on a raid or even in an xp group on red mobs. Once haste is factored in with a bard/chanter adding to the FBSS, I think the damage per second is better than the MoSS although I haven't wasted time logging pounding on the river source and/or done the math =P. With damage shields, I take less damage than I would with a faster weapon.

Timing debuffs and other spells between swings is nice too since each cast isn't putting off potential melee damage. You should think about it before you trade this weapon. Sometimes being #1 on the hate list is a bad thing, and playing an SK, you know it's very easy to build up agro with this class.
